title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s, and false otherwise. An anagram uses the same characters the same number of times.
hints: What property must two strings have if they're anagrams?
Count the frequency of each character in both strings and compare.
Use a hash map or array of size 26. Increment for s, decrement for t, check all are zero.
follow_ups: What if the inputs contain unicode characters?
Can you solve it using sorting?

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. Every open bracket must be closed by the same type and in the correct order.
hints: Think about what data structure naturally handles matching pairs in order.
A stack is perfect here — push opening brackets, pop for closing ones.
For each closing bracket, check if the top of the stack is its matching opener. Stack must be empty at the end.
follow_ups: What if you only had one type of bracket?
What's the minimum number of brackets to add to make it valid?

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
hints: A brute force checks all substrings. Can you do better with a sliding window?
Use a sliding window with a set to track characters in the current window.
Expand the right pointer. When you see a duplicate, shrink from the left until the duplicate is removed. Track max length.
follow_ups: What if you need the actual substring?
What if you're allowed at most k repeating characters?

title: Longest Palindromic Substring
description: Given a string s, return the longest palindromic substring in s.
hints: A palindrome mirrors around its center. How many possible centers are there?
Expand around each center — there are 2n-1 centers (each char + each gap between chars).
For each center, expand outward while characters match. Track the longest palindrome found.
follow_ups: Can you solve it in O(n) using Manacher's algorithm?

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given two strings s and t, return the minimum window substring of s that contains all characters of t (including duplicates). If no such window exists, return an empty string.
hints: This is a sliding window problem. What do you need to track about the characters in your window?
Use two hash maps — one for t's character counts, one for the current window. Expand right to include characters, shrink left to minimize.
Track how many unique characters have met their required count. When all are met, try shrinking from left. Update the minimum window.
follow_ups: What if you need to find all minimum windows?
